column1: # Debris cloud measured in the sensor / RIC frame of the reference satellite
# defined by [initial_state] in input.toml. The GUI's "Import RIC..." button
# (in the [batch] section) converts this file to ICRF and writes the
# resolved CSV that spody.exe consumes. See README.md.
# Columns:
#   id        : case name; per-case output files use it as a suffix
#   dr_x_km   : radial offset    (along  r_hat = r_ref / |r_ref|)
#   dr_y_km   : in-track offset  (along  s_hat orthogonal to r in the orbit plane)
#   dr_z_km   : cross-track off. (along  c_hat = (r_ref x v_ref) / |r_ref x v_ref|)
#   dv_x_kms  : radial   velocity  (same sensor-frame snapshot convention)
#   dv_y_kms  : in-track velocity
#   dv_z_kms  : cross-track velocity
#   am_srp    : passed through to debris.am_srp via [batch.columns]
#   Cr        : passed through to debris.Cr     via [batch.columns]
# Convention: snapshot / sensor-frame -- the GUI does NOT add an omega x r
# term to the velocity. dv components are treated as plain vector
# projections onto the instantaneous RIC axes (i.e. what an onboard sensor
# would report). See docstring of python/spody_gui/frames.py.
id
ref
lead
trail
high
low
side_p
side_n
